signature: pub trait Iterator { type Item; fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item>; }
description: The trait for dealing with iterators. Iterators are lazy and composable. Python equivalent: iterator protocol (__iter__, __next__).

methods.name: map
methods.signature: fn map<B>(self, f: FnMut(Self::Item) -> B) -> Map<Self, F>
methods.description: Transforms each element by applying a closure.

methods.name: filter
methods.signature: fn filter(self, predicate: FnMut(&Self::Item) -> bool) -> Filter<Self, P>
methods.description: Yields only elements that satisfy the predicate.

methods.name: collect
methods.description: Transforms an iterator into a collection.

methods.name: enumerate
methods.description: Creates an iterator that yields (index, value) pairs.

methods.name: fold
methods.signature: fn fold<B>(self, init: B, f: FnMut(B, Self::Item) -> B) -> B
methods.description: Reduces the iterator to a single value (like Python reduce).
methods.example: let sum = vec![1, 2, 3].iter().fold(0, |acc, x| acc + x);

methods.name: any
methods.signature: fn any(&mut self, f: FnMut(Self::Item) -> bool) -> bool
methods.description: Tests if any element matches the predicate.
